 {"id":"asupersync-3nweh","title":"Review Asupersync.lean for missing lemma slots (bd-330st support)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"LilacHill","created_at":"2026-02-04T03:43:34.391574793Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T03:44:35.646850460Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T03:44:35.646665967Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-3nweh","depends_on_id":"asupersync-6r78u","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:12Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-3nxv0","title":"[LEAN-COVERAGE][T5] Standardize proof artifact schema, manifesting, and retention","description":"Standardize artifact formats and retention for Lean verification runs.

Scope:
- Manifest schema for build inputs, toolchain, proofs, and results.
- Stable naming/versioning for artifacts.
- Retention/expiry policy for forensic reproducibility.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Artifacts from identical runs are stable.
- Manifest is sufficient for deterministic repro.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Artifact schema/versioning is explicit and validated in tests.
2. Manifest captures toolchain, commands, inputs, outputs, and comparability keys for deterministic repro.
3. Retention/expiry policy balances forensic reproducibility and storage hygiene.
4. Schema is consumed by CI triage routing and repro-bundle generation.","notes":"Artifact standardization is required so every failure/regression can be reconstructed reliably.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"DustyBay","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:20:31.616569312Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-15T17:02:08.930820552Z","closed_at":"2026-02-15T17:02:00.293587079Z","close_reason":"Completed","external_ref":"bd-8vpa5","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-3nxv0","depends_on_id":"asupersync-14tr8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:44Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-3nxv0","depends_on_id":"asupersync-honkj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:44Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1585,"issue_id":"asupersync-3nxv0","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed acceptance pass for bd-8vpa5: standardized artifact contract is now consumed by CI for smoke/full routing and full repro manifest generation. Added/verified explicit artifact contract fields (schema version, required manifest fields, retention bounds), profile triage + bundle metadata consumption in .github/workflows/ci.yml, and stronger contract tests in tests/lean_ci_verification_profiles.rs. Validation: rch exec -- cargo test -p asupersync --test lean_ci_verification_profiles -- --nocapture (pass, 7/7); rch exec -- cargo check --all-targets (pass); rch exec -- cargo fmt --check (pass). Workspace-wide rch exec -- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings remains failing due unrelated pre-existing workspace errors in many test/bench targets.","created_at":"2026-02-15T17:01:50Z"}]}
+{"id":"asupersync-3o03d","title":"[audit][combinator] Make Bracket fail closed after terminal completion","description":"src/combinator/bracket.rs line 206 uses unreachable!() in BracketPhase::Done, which panics if the Bracket future is polled after completion. Should return a typed BracketError::PolledAfterCompletion instead of panicking.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-12T07:16:10.659883821Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T07:35:01.990323395Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T07:35:01.990291326Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","combinator"]}
 {"id":"asupersync-3o1ga","title":"[LEAN-COVERAGE][T6.4c] Measure correctness regression rate vs proof/conformance gate maturity","description":"Track correctness regressions and rework burden as proof and conformance gates mature.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Regression trend line shows whether proof coverage investment is reducing defects.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Correctness regression trend is measured against conformance/proof gate maturity.
2. Rework burden and time-to-diagnosis metrics are reported with reproducible queries.
3. Results inform workflow/governance adjustments.","notes":"Claimed by ScarletOtter after closing asupersync-2ue65; starting correctness regression trend measurement vs proof/conformance maturity.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ScarletOtter","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:22:35.751258814Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T19:07:57.645764556Z","closed_at":"2026-02-16T19:07:57.645739028Z","close_reason":"Completed: correctness trend, reproducible query contract, and governance adjustment contract delivered.","external_ref":"bd-hr8i1","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-3o1ga","depends_on_id":"asupersync-3gf4i","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1724,"issue_id":"asupersync-3o1ga","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed `asupersync-3o1ga` (Track-6 T6.4c correctness regression trend vs proof/conformance maturity).

Delivered:
- `formal/lean/coverage/proof_impact_closed_loop_report_v1.json`
  - Added `report_snapshot.correctness_delta_evidence` with:
    - baseline/current maturity snapshots (`maturity_milestones`) linked to proof/conformance gate maturity.
    - explicit correctness trend deltas for:
      - `regressions_per_100_changes`
      - `rework_hours_per_regression`
      - `time_to_diagnosis_hours`
    - reproducible query hooks via `repro_queries` (`rch exec -- ...`).
    - governance feedback hooks via `governance_adjustments`.
    - confidence caveats for trend interpretation.
- `tests/lean_proof_impact_closed_loop_report.rs`
  - Extended machine checks to enforce correctness trend contract integrity:
    - milestone coverage ratio alignment with correctness metric row
    - delta arithmetic and non-regression trend expectations
    - reproducible query command format
    - governance adjustment owner-bead validity
- `formal/lean/coverage/README.md`
  - Documented correctness trend and governance-query contract in the Track-6 closed-loop section.

Validation (`rch`):
- `cargo test --test lean_proof_impact_closed_loop_report -- --nocapture` => PASS (4 passed)
- `cargo test --test proof_aware_workflow_contract -- --nocapture` => PASS (3 passed)
- `cargo test --test runtime_state_refinement_map -- --nocapture` => PASS (5 passed)

Previously established workspace gate status in this same session window:
- `cargo fmt --check` => FAIL due pre-existing unrelated formatting in `src/combinator/circuit_breaker.rs`.
- `cargo check --all-targets` => PASS (warnings only in unrelated test targets).
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` => FAIL due pre-existing unrelated lint backlog (`src/sync/barrier.rs`, `src/combinator/*`, `src/distributed/*`, etc.).

Acceptance criteria mapping:
1. Correctness regression trend is explicitly measured against proof/conformance gate maturity (`maturity_milestones` + gate ratio alignment).
2. Rework burden and time-to-diagnosis metrics are reported with reproducible queries (`repro_queries`).
3. Governance/workflow feedback is encoded as machine-readable `governance_adjustments` and validated in tests.
","created_at":"2026-02-16T19:07:40Z"}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-3o1rs","title":"[RAPTORQ-RFC6330][A1] Build RFC 6330 Clause-to-Code Traceability Matrix","description":"Create a requirement matrix that maps each implemented RFC 6330 requirement (table lookup, tuple generation, degree mapping, equation construction, decode behavior) to exact source files/functions/tests.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Matrix maps each in-scope RFC rule to code location(s), test(s), and status.
2. Missing or partial mappings are explicitly marked with owner and follow-up bead.
3. Matrix is deterministic and diff-friendly.
4. Test Mapping Requirement: matrix includes explicit mapping from RFC clauses to required comprehensive unit tests, deterministic E2E scenarios, and structured logging assertions.","notes":"Completed 2026-02-14: added docs/raptorq_rfc6330_clause_matrix.md with RFC 6330 clause-to-code/test/status matrix, explicit follow-up bead links, determinism/diff-friendliness rules, and concise glossary.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ScarletMarsh","estimated_minutes":240,"created_at":"2026-02-14T19:17:42.396630218Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T20:38:47.856711435Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T20:38:47.856650231Z","close_reason":"Completed matrix artifact at docs/raptorq_rfc6330_clause_matrix.md","external_ref":"bd-2wlqm","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analysis","raptorq","rfc6330"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-3o1rs","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1v5db","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-14T19:17:42.415267368Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
